name: Vashon High School
desc: Historic Black high school (1927). In tornado damage zone.

name: Sumner High School
desc: First Black high school west of the Mississippi (1875). Used as FEMA DRC.

name: Fontbonne University
desc: EF1-EF2 campus damage. Power poles snapped.

name: Washington University
desc: Trees/fences blown over. MetroLink overhead power damaged.

name: Centennial Christian Church
desc: Complete roof collapse trapping people. 911 redirected to non-emergency.

name: Union Tabernacle M.B. Church
addr: 626 N Newstead Ave, St. Louis, MO 63108

name: Forest Park
desc: 1,300 acres. Zoo had 10,000 guests — all survived.

name: O'Fallon Park
addr: 4397 W Florissant Ave, St. Louis, MO 63115
desc: Townhouses collapsed at park edge. EF2.

name: Saint Louis Zoo
desc: 10,000 guests during tornado. All survived. Butterfly enclosure damaged.

name: George B. Vashon Museum
addr: 2223 St. Louis Ave, St. Louis, MO 63106
desc: African American history museum. 4,000+ artifacts spanning 250 years. Founded by retired educator Calvin Riley in 2015. National Register of Historic Places.
